paragraphs.text: Words of blessing from Enoch with which he blessed the chosen and righteous who will exist in the day of tribulation, when all the wicked and ungodly are removed.
And Enoch answered and said: I was a righteous man whose eyes were opened by God, and who saw a vision of the Holy One in the heavens; this the angels showed me.
From them I heard everything, and I understood what I saw; but not for this generation, but for a distant generation that is to come.
Concerning the chosen I will speak, and regarding them I will utter my parable: The Holy and Great One will come forth from His dwelling.
The Eternal God will tread upon the earth, upon Mount Sinai, and will appear in the strength of His power from the heaven of heavens.
All will be afraid, and the Watchers will tremble, and great fear and dread will seize them to the ends of the earth.
The high mountains will be shaken, and the hills will sink down and melt like wax before the flame.
The earth will be flooded, and everything on it will perish, while judgment comes upon all.

But with the righteous He will make peace, and He will protect the chosen; mercy will be upon them. They will all belong to God, and they will prosper and be blessed. The splendor of God will illuminate them.

Observe all things that take place in heaven, how they do not change their courses; observe the luminaries of heaven, how they all rise and set in order, each in its season, without transgressing their appointed order.
Observe the earth and pay attention to the things that are done upon it, from beginning to end, how no work of God changes in its appearance.
Observe the summer and the winter, how then the whole earth is filled with water, and clouds and dew and rain rest upon it.

Observe and see how in winter all the trees appear as though they had withered and shed all their leaves, except fourteen trees which do not lose their foliage but retain the old leaves for two to three years until the new ones come.

And again observe the days of summer, when the sun shines upon it from the beginning. Seek shade and refuge from the heat of the sun, and the earth burns with scorching heat, and you cannot tread upon the ground or upon a rock because of the heat.
